name: FrontMCP
what_it_does: FrontMCP — a framework or MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with frontend web development workflows — providing tools for component generation, CSS manipulation, React/Vue/Svelte scaffolding, browser preview, and frontend build tooling integration, enabling AI-driven frontend development assistance and code generation.
use_cases: Generating React/Vue/Svelte components from design specs from frontend agents
Scaffolding frontend project structures from project setup agents
Applying CSS and styling changes to web components from design agents
Integrating AI assistance into frontend build pipelines from DevOps agents
Automating frontend testing and validation from QA agents
Accelerating frontend development with AI code generation from productivity agents
not_for: Teams not using modern JavaScript frameworks
best_when: A developer agent needs to work with modern frontend web development — generating components, scaffolding projects, or integrating AI into frontend build workflows.
avoid_when: Your work is primarily backend, mobile, or you prefer backend-first MCP tools.

auth.notes: No authentication — local development tool. Operates on local project files via stdio.

pricing.notes: Free, open source community MCP for frontend development.

known_agent_gotchas: Framework-specific support may be limited — verify React/Vue/Svelte coverage
Community MCP from individual contributor — maintenance may be irregular
Generated code quality depends on underlying model — review before committing
Local project files modified directly — use version control before running
Tool scope may have evolved since stars were counted — check current README
TypeScript/Node.js runtime required — ensure correct version installed
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local process. No external API calls. No credentials required. Modifies local project files — use git. Community MCP.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local process. No external calls. Modifies project files — use git for safety.
